title: eriogonum caninum
intro: Eriogonum caninum, also known as coyote tobacco or coyote's teeth, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum caninum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to help relieve respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
